title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases
description: An asynchronous job queue keeps aliases after cancellation and occasionally corrupts a newly allocated job at the same address. Learners cumulatively produce generation-checked handle repair through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
